text: The Iran war has now entered its third day, with Hezbollah launching missiles at Israel from Lebanon and Israel striking back with a fresh campaign across southern Lebanon.
Iran fired a massive retaliatory missile barrage at Israel, breaking through air defenses and killing eight people in the city of Beit Shemesh.
Three American service members have been killed so far, with President Trump warning that casualties will likely climb higher as Operation Epic Fury continues.
Oil prices surged nearly ten percent, with Brent crude approaching eighty dollars a barrel as tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has nearly stopped.
Tehran has declared it will not negotiate with the United States and has formed a temporary three-person leadership council after the death of Supreme Leader Khamenei.
With Hezbollah now actively engaged, this conflict has expanded far beyond Iran's borders.
The disruption of oil shipping through the Strait of Hormuz threatens a global energy crisis, and Iran's refusal to negotiate suggests this war is far from over.
